headline: Population's cadmium overexposure requires urgent action, French food agency tells government
description: In a report published on Wednesday, France's National Agency for Food Safety recommends drastically reducing levels of this carcinogenic metal in phosphate fertilizers and limiting the consumption of cereals, bread and pasta.

headline: Trump sends peace plan as Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to 'non-hostile' oil vessels
French conservatives to vote on whether to hold a presidential primary
Danish PM Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats lead election but fall short of majority
description: With all votes counted, the left-wing bloc was credited with 84 seats in the 179-seat parliament and the right with 77. Frederikson, a favorite going into elections, has been praised for her leadership after fending off US President Trump's repeated demands to annex Greenland, a Danish autonomous territory.

headline: Why a former Frontex chief is under investigation for complicity in crimes against humanity
description: Fabrice Leggeri, now an MEP for the far-right Rassemblement National, is accused by several nonprofits of having facilitated or covered up, during his tenure, the pushback of migrant boats by Greek authorities to Turkey, as well as the interception of vessels by Libyan coast guards attempting to reach Italy.

headline: Lionel Jospin, the man at the crossroads of France's 'irreconcilable lefts'
description: A moral compass for all the leading figures of the left and the architect of sweeping welfare reforms, the former prime minister was praised by the Socialists. Jean-Luc Mélenchon also admired him and could not stand his supporters criticizing Jospin.

headline: The serial-poisoning mother and daughter inspired by 'Breaking Bad'
description: Castor beans, wolfsbane, antifreeze, rat poison: Carole D. and Amélie D. considered every possible method to poison the former's partner, Enrique B. The two women are set to stand trial before a criminal court in western France starting March 25.

Emmanuel Grégoire's long run to Paris City Hall
description: The next mayor of Paris, who will be officially elected by the city council on Sunday, will now step into the spotlight after years in the shadow of his political mentors.

headline: Nestlé goes on trial over 'immeasurable' plastic pollution of water bottle dumps
description: The food industry giant is on trial before a criminal illegally dumping plastic bottles that contaminated the soil and water resources at 'exorbitant' levels.

headline: TotalEnergies bows to Trump administration, dropping two offshore wind projects
description: The French company is ending two offshore wind turbine programs off the coasts of New York and North Carolina, totaling 4 gigawatts of power. It will receive a $928 million reimbursement on the condition that the funds are reinvested in hydrocarbon projects.
